refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_timing_overhead budget.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2a: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical response times and operator control loop latency through hardware tuning and network optimization; monitor cycle time via telemetry.

DP-2b: Implement a hierarchical control architecture with local vehicle controllers executing time-critical sub-cycles autonomously while teleoperation provides high-level tasking; enforce safety constraints via a dedicated safety layer with bounded latency.

DP-2c: Allocate explicit cycle time budgets to vehicle dynamics (load/dump mechanics), travel (propulsion), operator control loop (teleoperation latency), and safety overhead; design each subsystem to meet its budget; measure and report actual vs. budgeted times via telemetry.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2c: Allocate explicit cycle time budgets to vehicle dynamics, travel, operator control loop (teleoperation latency), and safety overhead; design each subsystem to meet its budget; measure and report actual vs. budgeted times via telemetry.

potential_sources: Safety constraints may impose latency overhead that conflicts with cycle time targets; mitigation: allocate explicit safety_timing_overhead budget and design safety layer for bounded latency.
Teleoperation network latency is a shared resource; mitigation: establish latency_budget and monitor via Telemetry interface.
Vehicle dynamics (load/dump mechanics) and travel (propulsion) are coupled; mitigation: allocate independent budgets and measure via telemetry to identify coupling effects.
coupling_check.notes: DP-2c decouples the problem by introducing explicit budget allocation and telemetry-based measurement, enabling root-cause analysis of cycle time shortfalls. This supports iterative refinement at lower levels.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined by customer; Risk: Ambiguous targets lead to unverifiable acceptance criteria.
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure can capture cycle time events with sufficient fidelity; Risk: Measurement error masks true performance.
Assumption: Safety constraints can be designed with bounded latency overhead; Risk: Safety requirements may force unacceptable cycle time penalties.
Assumption: Teleoperation network provides consistent latency ≤ latency_budget; Risk: Network congestion or outages degrade cycle time performance.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2c to explicitly define the budget allocation methodology (e.g., vehicle_dynamics_budget, travel_budget, teleoperation_latency_budget, safety_overhead_budget) and the telemetry KPIs (cycle time p95, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety activations). Establish acceptance criteria for each budget component.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. It owns the cycle time budget allocation logic, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports cycle time KPIs (p95, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety activations) to Site Manager and System Engineer for KPI verification and performance analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives high-level tasking (load→travel→dump cycle commands) from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts and constraints from Safety Services to enforce safety overhead budgets and adjust cycle time targets if needed.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution coordinates network resources (latency, bandwidth) across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to meet teleoperation_latency_budget.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of cycle time KPI orchestration, budget allocation, and telemetry aggregation. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) report to NGHE Solution for KPI synthesis. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry reporting crosses boundary.

Vehicle owns vehicle_dynamics_budget and travel_budget execution, but cycle time KPI is a system-level outcome that requires coordination with Teleoperation and Safety Services. Assigning IM to Vehicle alone would diffuse teleoperation latency and safety overhead concerns.

Teleoperation owns teleoperation_latency_budget, but cycle time KPI depends equally on vehicle dynamics, travel, and safety constraints. Assigning IM to Teleoperation alone would diffuse vehicle and safety concerns.

Safety Services owns safety_overhead_budget, but cycle time KPI is not primarily a safety concern. Assigning IM to Safety Services would diffuse vehicle and teleoperation concerns and create ownership confusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it is the integration point where vehicle dynamics, teleoperation latency, and safety constraints converge to produce the cycle time KPI. NGHE Solution owns the budget allocation logic, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) execute their allocated budgets and report performance via Telemetry interface.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if it owns too much logic; mitigation: NGHE Solution should delegate budget execution to sub-modules and focus on orchestration and telemetry aggregation.

Risk: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if NGHE Solution requires high-frequency cycle time updates; mitigation: define telemetry sampling strategy (e.g., aggregate p95 over 100-cycle windows) to reduce bandwidth.

Risk: Network Link interface may not provide sufficient latency guarantees; mitigation: establish SLA for Network Link latency and include latency_budget in Tasking interface to allow dynamic adjustment.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the ownership boundary: NGHE Solution owns cycle time KPI orchestration and telemetry aggregation, but delegates budget execution to Vehicle (vehicle_dynamics_budget, travel_budget), Teleoperation (teleoperation_latency_budget), and Safety Services (safety_overhead_budget). Define explicit interfaces for budget reporting (e.g., sub-cycle duration reports from Vehicle, latency reports from Teleoperation, safety activation reports from Safety Services) to enable KPI synthesis.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2c (budget allocation + telemetry measurement) directly addresses FR-2 (cycle time KPI achievement). The DP decouples the problem by introducing explicit budgets for vehicle dynamics, travel, teleoperation latency, and safety overhead, enabling root-cause analysis and iterative refinement. The DP is verifiable via telemetry-based measurement of p95 cycle time and sub-cycle durations.

diffusion_risks: Risk: Budget execution is diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; mitigation: NGHE Solution owns budget allocation logic and telemetry aggregation, while sub-modules execute budgets and report performance.
Risk: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck; mitigation: define telemetry sampling strategy and aggregate p95 over 100-cycle windows.
Risk: Network Link interface may not provide sufficient latency guarantees; mitigation: establish SLA for Network Link latency and include latency_budget in Tasking interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary with containment_score=95. Budget execution is delegated to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) via explicit interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Minimal diffusion: only telemetry reporting and tasking coordination cross boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is verifiable via telemetry-based measurement of p95 cycle time and sub-cycle durations. Telemetry interface enables real-time monitoring and post-hoc analysis. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) are measurable and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution (orchestration and telemetry aggregation), with sub-budgets allocated to Vehicle (vehicle_dynamics_budget, travel_budget), Teleoperation (teleoperation_latency_budget), and Safety Services (safety_overhead_budget). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-2c to explicitly define budget allocation methodology and telemetry KPIs (cycle time p95, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety activations).
Clarify NGHE Solution ownership boundary: focus on orchestration and telemetry aggregation, delegate budget execution to sub-modules.
Define explicit interfaces for budget reporting (e.g., sub-cycle duration reports from Vehicle, latency reports from Teleoperation, safety activation reports from Safety Services).
Establish SLA for Network Link latency and include latency_budget in Tasking interface to allow dynamic adjustment.
Define telemetry sampling strategy (e.g., aggregate p95 over 100-cycle windows) to reduce bandwidth and enable real-time monitoring.

decision.justification: FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs (vehicle dynamics, travel, teleoperation latency, safety overhead). DP-2c (budget allocation + telemetry measurement) is sound and passes FR-DP and DP-IM gates. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary with high containment_score=95. Proceed to next decomposition level to refine sub-FRs (FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4) and allocate budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets (vehicle_dynamics_budget, travel_budget).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_timing_overhead budget.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events) for KPI verification.

assumptions: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined by customer.
Telemetry infrastructure can capture cycle time events with sufficient fidelity (±10ms or better).
Safety constraints can be designed with bounded latency overhead (safety_timing_overhead_budget).
Teleoperation network provides consistent latency ≤ latency_budget under agreed duty cycle conditions.
Vehicle dynamics (load/dump mechanics) and travel (propulsion) can be independently budgeted and measured.
Site Manager and System Engineer have access to telemetry data for KPI verification and performance analysis.
open_risks: Safety criticality (high) may impose unacceptable cycle time penalties; mitigation: allocate explicit safety_timing_overhead_budget and design safety layer for bounded latency.
Teleoperation network latency may exceed latency_budget under congestion or outages; mitigation: establish SLA for Network Link latency and include latency_budget in Tasking interface.

Measurement error in telemetry may mask true cycle time performance; mitigation: validate telemetry accuracy via independent measurement (e.g., on-site timing) and establish confidence intervals for p95 estimates.
Customer may not accept cycle time target if safety overhead is too high; mitigation: conduct trade-off analysis and present options to customer early in design.
evidence_needed: Formal definition of CT_target and duty cycle from customer.
Telemetry infrastructure specification (sampling rate, accuracy, latency).
Safety constraint analysis and safety_timing_overhead_budget estimate.
Network Link SLA (latency, bandwidth, availability) for teleoperation.
Vehicle dynamics and travel sub-cycle timing estimates (from Vehicle module).
Teleoperation latency budget estimate (from Teleoperation module).
Safety overhead budget estimate (from Safety Services module).
Acceptance criteria for each budget component (vehicle_dynamics_budget, travel_budget, teleoperation_latency_budget, safety_overhead_budget).
